Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–375, div. A, title IX, § 913(b)118 Stat. 2028Oct. 28, 2004“Subsection (a) of , United States Code, as added by subsection (a), shall apply with respect to fiscal years after fiscal year 2005.”section 2273a of title 10

United States Policy on Operationally Responsive Space, , , provided that:Pub. L. 109–364, div. A, title IX, § 913(a)120 Stat. 2355Oct. 17, 2006“It is the policy of the United States to demonstrate, acquire, and deploy an effective capability for operationally responsive space to support military users and operations from space, which shall consist of—“(1)responsive satellite payloads and busses built to common technical standards;“(2)low-cost space launch vehicles and supporting range operations that facilitate the timely launch and on-orbit operations of satellites;“(3)responsive command and control capabilities; and“(4)concepts of operations, tactics, techniques, and procedures that permit the use of responsive space assets for combat and military operations other than war.”

Joint Operationally Responsive Space Payload Technology Organization, , , which directed the Secretary of Defense to establish or designate an organization in the Department of Defense to coordinate joint operationally responsive space payload technology, was repealed by , , .Pub. children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: There is within the Space Force a program office known as the Space Rapid Capabilities Office (in this section referred to as the “Office”). The facilities of the Office may not be co-located with the headquarters facilities of the Space Force Space Systems Command.

children.heading: .—Head of Office
children.label: b .—Head of Office
children.text: The head of the Office shall be the designee of the Secretary of the Air Force. The head of the Office shall report to the Chief of Space Operations.

to contribute to the development of low-cost, rapid reaction payloads, busses, launch, and launch control capabilities in order to fulfill joint military operational requirements for on-demand space support and reconstitution;
to coordinate and execute space rapid capabilities efforts across the Department of Defense with respect to planning, acquisition, and operations; and
to rapidly develop and field new classified space capabilities.
The Secretary of the Air Force shall designate the acquisition executive of the Office who shall provide streamlined acquisition authorities for projects of the Office.
The Joint Capabilities Integration and Development System process shall not apply to acquisitions by the Office.

children.label: A
children.text: there are separate, dedicated unclassified and classified program elements for space rapid capabilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Office executes the responsibilities of the Office through such program elements.
The Office shall manage the program elements required by paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Board of Directors
children.label: f .—Board of Directors
children.text: The Secretary of the Air Force shall establish for the Office a Board of Directors (to be known as the “Space Rapid Capabilities Board of Directors”) to provide coordination, oversight, and approval of projects of the Office.
